reasoning: Initial observation suggests a linear relationship between position, velocity, and time. To confirm this, I will increase the time and observe the change in position.
The position increased by 5 meters when time increased by 1 second, confirming a linear relationship. I will now increase the velocity to further confirm the equation.

The position increased linearly with both time and velocity. Increasing the velocity while keeping time constant resulted in a proportional increase in position, confirming the linear relationship. With the initial position set to 0, the velocity of 10 m/s over 3 seconds results in a position of 30 meters, which matches the observation. Therefore, the equation can be derived.
